what_it_does: Designs the coordination methodology for building the Concurrent Cognition feature via sub-agent-driven development: how the loop-prep triad becomes a shared reference, how work is sliced into file-disjoint parallel lanes and a sequential suite.py spine, how sub-agents reference the plan and report back, and how the build is phased and verified.
observations: The doc opens with three load-bearing decisions: slice by file-disjointness not feature-verticals, specialize an existing proven build loop rather than invent one, and treat graph-recursive self-build as an after not a bootstrap.
Most net-new pieces collide on runtime/suite.py and runtime/bridge.py, forcing a sequential spine S0-S6 rather than many parallel verticals.
Three genuinely file-disjoint lanes can run in parallel: Lane T (fabric/transport.py json_schema), Lane V (nodes/llm.py VOLATILE), and Lane R (canvas/app/src front-end).
The loop-prep triad (Completion Criteria, Implementation Guide, Research Synthesis) is used as durable shared state because sub-agents cannot message each other.
Verification is two-faced (FUNCTION + FORM), done by a separate verifier/design-critic, with no self-passing.
The repo forbids branches and per-agent worktrees; concurrent commits are safe only when lanes are file-disjoint.
A cognition-build skill is proposed as a specialization of six existing sibling build skills.
Full recursion (coordinating the build through the company engine graph) is blocked until the engine has parallel dispatch, which this build itself adds in S1.
AGENTS.md rules 2, 3, 4, 8, 9, and 10 are explicitly honored and made specific.
Known open forks are deliberately not decided and are routed to Tim.
standouts: File-disjointness, not feature-verticals, is the real constraint on build parallelism.
The 32-way runtime cognition does not imply a 32-way parallel build.
Recursion through the company engine is an AFTER, not the bootstrap coordination method.
No branches and no per-agent worktrees: all work commits to one branch in the existing worktree.
conventions: Follows the loop-prep research -> synthesis -> guide -> criteria change-flow.
Follows AGENTS.md rules: no branches, verified-before-commit, two-faced criteria, preserve-lists, fail loud, surface gaps rather than invent.
Specializes an existing proven sibling build-loop pattern instead of creating a parallel coordination system.
Breaks the orchestrating-swarms default of per-agent worktrees/branches by relying on file-disjoint lanes and a single branch.
concerns: Heavy centralization on runtime/suite.py makes the sequential spine a bottleneck and raises blast-radius for the chat() part-loop restructuring in S4.
Lane R has a split completion: static build and design-lint can finish early, but by-use FORM verification must wait for S6, creating a timing-trap risk.
The plan depends on the triad being expansive enough to prevent seam drift between parallel agents.
Many open forks are deferred to Tim, so unresolved decisions could block the spike and later phases.
notes: A meta-build plan that maps how to coordinate sub-agents to construct Concurrent Cognition, emphasizing file-disjoint parallel lanes, a serial suite.py spine, and independent no-self-passing verification.
questions: What are the exact acceptance suite names and commands for the new cognition capabilities?
Which specific canvas/app/src files constitute Lane R?
What is the precise content of the cognition.* SSE contract that Lane R and S6 both build against?
How are the six existing build skills structured, and which exact prompts/tools does cognition-build inherit?
What is the fallback if Lane T (json_schema) does not land before the proving spike?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to design HOW the build is coordinated, not WHAT is built; the file does exactly that, mapping methodology, phases, lanes, and verification.
apparent_themes: Shared durable state via files replaces inter-agent messaging.
File-disjointness is the true boundary of safe build parallelism.
No self-passing: independent verification is required for both function and form.
Bootstrap order matters: build the parallel mechanism before using it recursively.
Preserve the live system while extending it, especially the chat() single-call path.
Surface gaps and route hard decisions to a human rather than silently choosing.
intention_signals: It is trying to prevent the common failure modes of parallel agent builds—file collisions, self-graded work, premature greening, and recursive bootstrapping traps—by translating a proven sibling build loop into a cognition-specific coordination plan.
novelty: Distinctive in explicitly deriving build parallelism from a collision map of net-new code rather than from the architecture's runtime parallelism, and in treating full graph-recursive self-build as a post-bootstrap milestone rather than the coordination method.
contribution: Provides a concrete, file-aware coordination map (parallel lanes versus a serial spine), a verification model with independent verifiers and design-critics, and a bootstrap-safe recursion stance that a fused build plan can reuse to sequence and guard a multi-agent implementation.
summary_for_embedding: B5 is a meta-design document that lays out the sub-agent-driven build coordination plan for the Concurrent Cognition feature, including file-disjoint parallel lanes, a sequential suite.py spine, the loop-prep triad as shared reference, independent verification, and phased integration.
intention_for_embedding: Written to guide a multi-sub-agent implementation by mapping which pieces can be built in parallel, which must be serial, how agents reference the plan, and how completion is verified without self-passing.
